item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Hello Doctor Cimmerian, my apologies for the less than prompt reply ;-P The image is actually 100% mine, I shot it with a small compact camera along a freeway. As such, there shouldn't be any copyright issue, but if a source is still necessary, I'm not sure how I'd go about providing it. Perhaps a quick peek at the un-cropped version? Thanks in advance! ;-D

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I was hesitant to say this should even be considered Keter, but I'm not entirely certain now. That said, it's a fun read and leaves a few questions that I think are more appealing than their answers. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
O.O

…Speechless. I think you've actually come up with a highly plausible explanation for why 3308 does what it does - most impressive. Funny thing, I work with a guy named Kenny and this is almost exactly how he'd sound in real life, lol X-D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kenny walks up to front loader parked on 45-degree slope and climbs in. He sees the note and reads it.

Kenny, muttering to himself: "Damn it, Jim, I've got work to do and it has to be done today. If the brake needs work anyway, Ralph can just replace the whole damned thing for all I care."

Kenny goes about his work, albeit a bit more slowly than normal and accompanied by loud squeals from the brake.

Later that day, having finished his work, Kenny parks the front loader on level terrain and goes home.

The next morning, somewhere, deep down in the loader, a fluttering almost-thought awakens. Something is wrong. The loader was supposed to remain parked until it could be repaired. It is parked now, but something has changed. Something is different. Something is WRONG!
The loader is a good loader, and always does its work in the right and proper way. It has to fix this! It has to put things back the way they were, so that it can be repaired and be a good loader again!
It will fix this! All it needs to do is put in just…a little…effort…and…

There. All better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's what Antarctica is for.

Or the moon.

Or orbit. It doesn't matter how heavy the thing gets it's in orbit… WAIT nevermind, eventually it might start affecting tides or causing its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you could technically just make a complicated cover story with plate tectonics in the field…
Or you could, just, throw it in the ocean somewhere…
But then the weight plays in factor there…
I'm sure the Foundation will find something out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well explained! ;-D

Also, the open field idea could indeed work, though I imagine jetliners would still be able to see it from the air, no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I understand correctly, I think propping it up on a ramp would count as artificially suspending it. Ergo it would increase its mass until it either slid down or forced the ramp flat, and then ratchet itself and everything around it back to 45°.

Letting it tilt an empty field in North Dakota and never moving it again might work, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, thank you very much for the compliment ;-D I know I don't go into real specifics about the zone of effect, but should I venture an estimation, there would probably be somewhere in the ballpark of several square km affected by the tilt, not just roadways where 3308 happens to be parked. On the other hand, I figured putting an exact measurement on the area would end up taking away from the unpredictable nature of the object. As such, I feel the [DATA EXPUNGED] as well as the collateral damage sustained by residential locations and such leaves the scope of damage to the reader's imagination ;-) I'm willing to add something to help specify this concept, though, if it would help with clarity.

As to your second point, it's not a very pronounced point in the article, but there is mention that ramps able to support thousands of kg of weight also eventually collapse under 3308, so they would do no good either. Thing is just too stubborn I guess ;-P

Edit: Sorry, I just now realized you were referring to the tow truck and not the ramp ;-P In any event, being hooked up to a towing rig would still be a form of restraint or suspension in the case of 3308, so it would inevitably break away from the towing cables as well. Just figured I'd actually answer your question ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure I understand the containment procedures. Can't they just construct a 45° ramp somewhere and put 3308 on it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure the "breaks" in the last paragraph should be "brakes", not sure if that's minor enough for me to fix myself.

I like this overall but my favorite part was that it "increases its own density" until it breaks whatever it's contained in - it gave me a nice old-school series 1 feel of an object that isn't sentient but still somehow wants to cause chaos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quirky and original. And I do mean that: this is a great effect to have on a Keter object.

Two thoughts. First, though you do say in the article the area of effect isn't measurable, I'd like some indication of how much is going to be effected by this thing at any given time. If it's on a roadway, sure, some portion of that roadway will be tilted, but what if it's just sitting on open ground?

Second, for the containment procedures, why not just leave it attached to a tow truck, since that worked initially? From the description, I got the sense that it only increases its weight while suspended, or did I misread?
